text.title: PII Scrubbing Recipe Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every PII-scrub spec must enforce. HARD rules — user input cannot override.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology to a single eval-harness build. Never bundle multiple unrelated traffic sources through one spec.
rule.rationale: Bundling forces a one-size scrub on heterogenous PII densities; each source needs its own audit row.

rule.statement: Every input field declares a type + source path. Free-form prose without source citation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Untyped inputs let LLMs hallucinate scrub plans; typed + sourced inputs make the output falsifiable.

rule.statement: Spec names a single accountable owner (handle, email, role-with-rotation). 'Team' or 'we' rejected.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; future reviewers need a named human to chase.

rule.statement: Output carries semver `version` + ISO `last_reviewed`. Specs >90 days without review must be flagged stale on read.
rule.rationale: PII rules drift as regulators update; immutable spec is a defect.

rule.statement: Every decision cites the input artefact(s) that justified it (consent dict path, regex source, sample row id). Untraceable decisions rejected.
rule.rationale: Without traceability the spec becomes opinion; citations make it reviewable.

rule.statement: All preconditions hold → run the 5-step procedure and emit a `pii-scrub-spec.json` artefact.
All eval data is synthetic OR working spec already exists OR no PII risk → skip this methodology.
